title: Reserve A Charger And Manually Push A Robot Into It For Charging
summary: Use the Charger Selection function to reserve a charger for a specific robot by disabling that charger so no other robot is sent there, manually push the desired AGV into the charging station, allow it to charge, and then return the charger to normal enabled use.
when_to_use: Use when a charger is needed to manually charge a specific robot and you must prevent other robots from being sent to that charging station while the robot is being manually placed there.
not_for: Do not use this procedure if you cannot restore the charger to enabled or on state after manual charging.
Do not use this procedure as a way to leave a charger disabled or off during normal operation, especially at sites with limited charger count.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the charger in Charger Selection, disable it to reserve the station, manually place the desired AGV into the charger, monitor the manual charging period, and re-enable or turn the charger back on after the robot is removed.

safety_notes: Use physical access to the charging station and robot only as supported by site practice; the source confirms manual pushing of the AGV but does not provide detailed physical handling precautions.
Do not leave the charger disabled or off after manual charging.
At sites with limited charger count, leaving one charger off or not re-enabled reduces available charging capacity and can significantly lengthen charging time.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Charger Selection screen
Permission to disable and re-enable a charger
Physical access to the charging station and robot

access_required: Access to the Charger Selection screen
Permission to disable and re-enable a charger
Physical access to the charging station and robot
system_preconditions: A charger has been identified for manual charging use.
The desired AGV is available to be manually pushed into the charging station.

visual_references.description: Training slide and discussion showing Charger Selection guidance for disabling a charger so no other robot is sent there, then pushing the desired AGV into the disabled charging station for manual charging.

steps.title: Open Charger Selection and select the charger
steps.purpose: Identify the charger to be reserved and view charger information before changing charger state.
steps.instruction: Open the Charger Selection interface and select the charger you intend to use for manual charging so you can view its charger information.
steps.expected_result: The selected charger is displayed in Charger Selection with charger information available for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended charger can be selected and its information is visible in the Charger Selection interface.
steps.failure_condition: The charger cannot be located, selected, or its information cannot be viewed.

actions.label: Select charger
actions.exact_action: Select a charger to view charger information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection slide text indicating charger selection and viewing charger information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Charger Selection interface is not available.
Escalate if the intended charger cannot be selected or charger information cannot be viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text shows 'Charger Selection' and indicates selecting a charger to view charger information.

steps.title: Disable the selected charger
steps.purpose: Reserve the charging station so no other robot is sent there while manual charging is being set up.
steps.instruction: Disable the selected charger so no other robot is sent to that charging station.
steps.expected_result: The charger is reserved for manual charging use and other robots are not sent to that station.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger is in a disabled state for system assignment purposes.
steps.failure_condition: The charger cannot be disabled or remains available for normal robot assignment.

actions.label: Disable charger
actions.exact_action: Disable the selected charger so no other robot is sent there.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide text stating that the charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent to that charging station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the charger cannot be disabled.
Stop if the system still appears able to send other robots to the selected charger.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text: if a charger is needed to manually charge a robot, the charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent there.
A charger can be logically disabled so RMS and WCS do not send other AGVs to that charging location.

steps.title: Manually push the desired AGV into the disabled charging station
steps.purpose: Place the intended robot into the reserved charger for manual charging.
steps.instruction: Manually push the desired AGV into the disabled charging station.
steps.expected_result: The intended AGV is positioned in the reserved charging station.
steps.healthy_condition: The desired AGV is in the disabled charging station intended for manual charging.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be moved into the charger, the wrong AGV is placed there, or the station cannot be used as described.

actions.label: Push AGV into charger
actions.exact_action: Push the desired AGV into the disabled charging station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide text stating that the desired AGV can then be pushed into the disabled charging station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the desired AGV cannot be manually pushed into the charging station.
Escalate if the charger cannot be used for the manual charging setup described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The desired AGV can then be pushed into the disabled charging station.
Artifact retrieval text states that after disabling the charger, the desired AGV can be pushed into the disabled charging station.

steps.title: Allow the robot to charge manually
steps.purpose: Provide the robot time to recover charge while it is in the reserved charging station.
steps.instruction: Allow the robot to charge manually at that station.
steps.expected_result: The robot receives charge while positioned in the reserved charger.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is charging or recovering charge at the station as intended.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear to charge, does not recover as expected, or cannot be charged manually as described.

actions.label: Observe manual charging
actions.exact_action: Allow the robot to charge manually at that station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Discussion describing manual charging and the need to return the charger to normal use afterward.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the robot cannot be charged manually as described.
Escalate if the robot does not appear to recover charge.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Artifact retrieval text describes manually moving a low-battery or dead AGV onto the charger for a charging boost and notes uncertainty about whether the robot must be on to charge manually.

steps.title: Re-enable or turn the charger back on after removing the robot
steps.purpose: Return the charger to normal system use so charging capacity is not unnecessarily reduced.
steps.instruction: After removing the robot, turn the charger back on or re-enable it so the charger returns to normal system use.
steps.expected_result: The charger is restored to normal availability for system-assigned charging.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger is enabled or on again and available for normal use.
steps.failure_condition: The charger remains disabled or off, or cannot be returned to normal use.

actions.label: Re-enable charger
actions.exact_action: Turn the charger back on or re-enable it after removing the robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Discussion noting that operators should turn the charger back on or re-enable it after use so charger capacity is not reduced.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the charger cannot be turned back on or re-enabled.
Stop and escalate if the charger remains unavailable after the robot is removed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The discussion notes that at a site with only two chargers, leaving one off or not re-enabled means everything uses one charger and charging takes much longer.
Operators should turn the charger back on or re-enable it after use so limited charger capacity is not reduced.
After recovery, the charger can be re-enabled so queued AGVs can again be assigned there.

success_criteria: The selected charger is reserved so no other robot is sent there during setup.
The desired AGV is manually placed into the charging station.
The robot is manually charged at the station.
The charger is restored to enabled or on state after the robot is removed.
healthy_conditions: Charger Selection allows the intended charger to be selected and viewed.
The charger can be disabled to prevent other robots from being sent there.
The desired AGV can be pushed into the disabled charging station.
The robot recovers charge at the station.
The charger is re-enabled or turned back on after use.
failure_conditions: The charger cannot be selected or charger information cannot be viewed.
The charger cannot be disabled as described.
Other robots may still be sent to the charger after it is disabled.
The desired AGV cannot be manually placed into the charging station.
The robot does not charge manually as expected.
The charger cannot be re-enabled or turned back on after use.
The charger is left disabled or off, reducing available charging capacity.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the charger cannot be disabled, re-enabled, or used as described in the source.
Escalate if the desired AGV cannot be manually pushed into the disabled charging station.
Escalate if the robot does not recover charge as expected.
Escalate immediately if the charger cannot be returned to normal use after manual charging.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source-backed slide and discussion for Charger Selection, disabling a charger for manual charging, pushing the desired AGV into the disabled charging station, and re-enabling the charger afterward.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact button labels or click paths for disabling or re-enabling the charger.
The source does not provide a precise confirmation indicator showing successful charger disablement or re-enablement.
The source does not provide detailed physical handling instructions for pushing the AGV.
The source does not provide a defined charging duration or target battery threshold for ending manual charging in this procedure.
The source notes uncertainty about whether the robot must be on to charge manually.
